text: i put 0 to 5 dollars as the cost of a nutritious meal on campus, but the reality is that there is no such thing as a nutritious meal option on our campus.  we currently do not have a cafeteria on campus, so the only food options are snacks from vending machines and the bookstore.  these snacks just small snacks and not meals.  they are also highly processed foods, so if anyone wants to eat something nutritious, they must either bring it from home or eat off campus.  when we did have a small cafeteria on campus, there were not as many cheaper options on the menu.  prices were not too high, but there was not a variety of differently priced options for whole meals that were not snacks or sides.  there were also not very many nutritious options or options for people with dietary restrictions.  they were also not consistent with their hours, so sometimes people would come in that morning, but would not be able to have breakfast because it was closed.  this happened to me once before as well.  that being said, that food vendor is no longer on campus.  however, we have just started up a food pantry, so students will at least have some more options of obtaining food.  this is a good thing and i am happy that this change was implemented and it bodes well for our future.  we started a community garden as well, so as that grows there should not only be more food availability on campus, but also more nutritious options.  overall, i think we are improving a little in the area of food availability.
